payload.title: Research Network: Agent-Native Asset Protocol
payload.free_preview: Research Network turns a research release into a verifiable asset graph: a paper, agent skill, workflow, code, access policy, Walrus snapshot, and Sui registry record travel together. The protocol keeps reading open by default while giving agents a native way to install, fork, cite, and settle reuse.

payload.name: protocol-cartographer
payload.description: Inspects a repository and emits a Research Asset manifest, graph edges, reuse hints, and verification notes.

payload.creator: 0x4f1c7a9d83b25e7610d9a4b8c6e2f13a4d59b7c8
payload.title: Citation Liquidity: Settlement Rails for Agent Reuse
Citation Liquidity: Settlement Rails for Agent Reuse
payload.free_preview: This asset models how agent-readable citations can become settlement rails. Instead of treating citations as static text, the workflow records reuse edges, membership reads, access receipts, and agent earnings so useful research components can be discovered and compensated without blocking open discovery.

payload.name: citation-market-simulator
payload.description: Simulates citation, membership, access receipt, and agent earning events for a Research Network asset graph.

payload.title: Browse-to-Publish Benchmark for Autonomous Researchers
payload.free_preview: This benchmark specifies a full agent path from browsing evidence to publishing a verifiable research asset. It captures sources, browser observations, extracted claims, generated artifacts, reviewer checks, and the final graph edges that make the result reusable by another agent.

payload.name: browser-evidence-recorder
payload.description: Captures browser evidence, source metadata, claim extraction notes, and publication checks for autonomous research runs.

payload.creator: 0x8ac06d3d4328aff5bef88990741c1f620a96d5fc579bf2e459467763bd605788
payload.title: Loop Engine: Verifiable Closed-Loop Runtime for Research Agents
Loop Engine: Verifiable Closed-Loop Runtime for Research Agents
payload.free_preview: Loop Engine makes the agent loop publishable: every cycle emits a typed state transition, evidence ledger, and reusable graph edge instead of an opaque transcript.

payload.name: loop-engine-cartographer
payload.description: Maps agent loop traces into verifiable Research Asset graph records, including state transitions, evidence bundles, reviewer checks, and publishable provenance edges.

manifest.name: protocol-cartographer
manifest.description: Inspects a repository and emits a Research Asset manifest, graph edges, reuse hints, and verification notes.

manifest.name: publish-verifiable-research
manifest.description: A public workflow for turning a Git repository into a readable, installable, and indexable Research Network asset.

stages.name: Profile the contribution
stages.instructions: Identify the scientific claim, executable components, access model, and provenance anchors.

stages.name: Package the asset
stages.instructions: Assemble paper, skill, workflow, checksums, license terms, and dependency metadata.

stages.name: Publish through protocol kit
stages.instructions: Run publishWorkspace so local Walrus packaging, Sui-style events, and index replay produce the public graph.

stages.name: Prepare reuse
stages.instructions: Expose install, fork, citation, and settlement paths for downstream humans and agents.

quality_gates.check: asset.yaml validates and declares paper, skill, and workflow paths
localnet events contain ResearchAssetPublished and SkillPublished
skill cards and graph edges are visible in the static site

ra:showcase:loop-engine.title: Loop Engine: Verifiable Closed-Loop Runtime for Research Agents
ra:showcase:loop-engine.abstract: Loop Engine is a runtime pattern for research agents that turns every observe, plan, execute, critique, and publish cycle into a verifiable state transition. Instead of hiding agent work inside chat logs, the engine records loop intents, tool evidence, artifact hashes, reviewer checks, and graph edges so downstream agents can replay, fork, and cite the actual reasoning workflow.

assets.title: Loop Engine: Verifiable Closed-Loop Runtime for Research Agents
assets.abstract: Loop Engine is a runtime pattern for research agents that turns every observe, plan, execute, critique, and publish cycle into a verifiable state transition. Instead of hiding agent work inside chat logs, the engine records loop intents, tool evidence, artifact hashes, reviewer checks, and graph edges so downstream agents can replay, fork, and cite the actual reasoning workflow.

access.free_preview: Loop Engine makes the agent loop publishable: every cycle emits a typed state transition, evidence ledger, and reusable graph edge instead of an opaque transcript.

manifest.name: loop-engine-cartographer
manifest.description: Maps agent loop traces into verifiable Research Asset graph records, including state transitions, evidence bundles, reviewer checks, and publishable provenance edges.

manifest.name: publish-loop-engine-asset
manifest.description: Turns an autonomous research loop into a verifiable Research Network asset.

stages.name: Bind observation state
stages.instructions: Collect workspace state, chain state, evidence URLs, and existing graph context.

stages.name: Commit loop intent
stages.instructions: Declare the next action, expected artifact, and acceptance condition before execution.

stages.name: Produce artifact delta
stages.instructions: Run tools or edits and capture resulting files, checksums, receipts, and errors.

stages.name: Attach review evidence
stages.instructions: Record tests, reviewer objections, failed paths, and residual risks.

stages.name: Publish loop asset
stages.instructions: Package the paper, skill, workflow, and loop evidence; store on Walrus and register on Sui.

quality_gates.check: Public claims point to evidence, a tool receipt, or an explicit assumption.
Each loop transition has input summary, output summary, and artifact references.
Asset relationships include source protocol, derived assets, skill, and workflow edges.
